title: leucaena salvadorensis
intro: Leucaena salvadorensis, also known as salcedo or wild lead tree, is a species of flowering plant in the pea family Fabaceae. It is native to Central America and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant has various health benefits, including reducing fever, treating digestive issues, and alleviating respiratory problems, as well as providing relief from rheumatism and other inflammatory conditions. Leucaena salvadorensis is often prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and can also be used in the form of leaves or bark to make medicines and remedies.
